results.title: Strengthening the Macadamia Nuts Export Value Chain in Rwanda through Organic Certification – ELPS
eligible_activities: Supporting macadamia smallholder farmers in obtaining organic certification
results.proposal_summary: IFAD is re-opening the ELPS Call for Proposals 2025 to select an Implementing Partner for an intervention to strengthen the macadamia nuts export value chain in Rwanda through organic certification. This project aims to support macadamia smallholder farmers in obtaining organic certification by facilitating access to key inputs, required knowledge, and infrastructure. Local non-profit organizations, NGOs, and commercial entities are welcome to apply. The deadline for submissions is January 5, 2026. Grant size is not specified. There are no co-financing requirements mentioned.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call focuses on strengthening the macadamia nuts export value chain in Rwanda through organic certification, promoting sustainable enterprises, and improving conditions for smallholder farmers, which aligns with ILO's mandate on decent work in supply chains and sustainable development.

results.title: FO4IMPACT (Near East, North Africa, Europe and Central Asia)
results.proposal_summary: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is offering the FO4IMPACT grant to promote inclusive, sustainable, and climate-resilient agricultural value chains in the Near East, North Africa, Europe and Central Asia region. Eligible applicants are farmers’ organizations representing more than one country or non-profit entities. The grant aims to strengthen the capacity of farmers' organizations, and proposals are due by December 29, 2025. Co-financing requirements are not mandatory.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on sustainable enterprises and value chains in agriculture, along with the emphasis on strengthening farmers' organizations aligns with ILO's work on promoting decent work in supply chains and supporting sustainable economic development.

results.classification_summary: This is a call for proposals from IFAD for FO4IMPACT in Latin America and the Caribbean. The grant aims to promote inclusive, sustainable, and climate-resilient agricultural value chains and strengthen the capacity of FOs. Eligible applicants are legally registered Farmers’ Organizations (FOs) in an IFAD Member State operating regionally or across multiple countries, or a consortium led by a regional or multi-country FO, which may include other legally registered non-profit entities. A co-financing contribution of at least 20% (cash and/or in-kind) is recommended. Proposals must be submitted by 2025-12-03.

results.title: FO4IMPACT (Latin American and the Caribbean)
results.deadline_text: 3 December 2025, at 11:59 p.m. (Anywhere on Earth, UTC–12)
theme: Just Transitions towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies and Societies
eligible_activities: Promoting inclusive, sustainable, and climate-resilient agricultural value chains
Strengthening the capacity of FOs to deliver services to smallholder farmers
results.proposal_summary: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is launching a call for proposals for the FO4IMPACT regional grant in Latin America and the Caribbean. The grant aims to promote inclusive, sustainable, and climate-resilient agricultural value chains and strengthen the capacity of Farmers' Organizations (FOs) to deliver services to smallholder farmers, engage in policy dialogue, and improve governance. Farmers’ Organizations (FOs) registered in an IFAD Member State and operating regionally or across multiple countries can apply, or as a consortium led by a regional or multi-country FO including other non-profit entities. A 20% co-financing contribution is recommended. The deadline for submission is December 3, 2025.
results.ilo_match_evidence: Focus on sustainable enterprises, employment policy/job creation, and just transitions in agriculture aligns with ILO's mandate for decent work in rural economies.

results.title: Increase the impact of digital remittances in rural Morocco

application_process.description: Submission of Annex 1 and Annex 2 via email

eligible_activities: Expanding access to digital transfer services in rural and peri-urban areas
Promoting the use of savings, credit, and insurance products linked to remittances
results.proposal_summary: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD), through its Financing Facility for Remittances (FFR) and the DigitRemit Morocco programme, is launching a call for proposals to increase the impact of digital remittances on financial inclusion, economic empowerment, and sustainable development in rural and peri-urban areas of Morocco. The objectives include expanding access to digital transfer services, promoting the use of financial services linked to remittances, and strengthening financial and digital education. Eligible applicants include private sector, public sector entities, and international organizations. The deadline for submission is December 10, 2025. Co-financing requirements are not specified in the provided text.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call focuses on financial inclusion, skills development and economic empowerment through digital remittances, which aligns with ILO's focus on employment and decent work.

results.title: Strengthening the macadamia nut export value chain in Rwanda
results.proposal_summary: IFAD is seeking expressions of interest from private sector companies to strengthen the macadamia nut export value chain in Rwanda under the Enhanced Linkages between Private sector and Small-scale producers (ELPS) initiative. The aim is to forge partnerships between large corporations and small-scale producers to promote sustainable and productive farming practices. Companies interested in contributing resources to support farmers, enhance livelihoods, and promote sustainable production are invited to apply. The deadline for submissions is October 13, 2025. No specific funding amount or implementation period is mentioned. The call targets food, agriculture, and trading companies.
results.ilo_match_evidence: Focus on sustainable production practices and enhancing livelihoods of farmers in the supply chain aligns moderately with ILO's goals around decent work in supply chains and sustainable enterprises.

results.exclusion_reason: ILO is not an eligible applicant type according to Annex 2 and 3 of the call for proposals. The Annexes provide self-certification forms for eligibility that do not include International Organizations.

results.title: Livestock and Communities for One Health in the Mekong (LIVECOM)
eligible_activities: piloting community initiatives on disease prevention
piloting community initiatives on antimicrobial resistance
piloting community initiatives on food safety
piloting community initiatives on environmental health
generating knowledge products and lessons learned
results.proposal_summary: IFAD's LIVECOM grant aims to bolster community-level capacity and regional teamwork concerning One Health across Thailand, Cambodia, Viet Nam, and Laos. The initiative seeks to support projects focused on preventing disease, tackling antimicrobial resistance, ensuring food safety, and promoting environmental health, with a special focus on the involvement of women, youth, and marginalized communities. The deadline for submitting proposals is October 6, 2025. ILO isn't eligible to apply. No information is provided regarding co-financing.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on livestock, food safety and rural communities has a tangential connection to ILO's mandate on working conditions and sustainable enterprises, but this call lacks a direct and substantial focus on labour.

results.classification_summary: This is a call for proposals from IFAD and the government of Somalia seeking proposals from women entrepreneurs across rural Somalia who want to grow their businesses by improving their market access. The challenge aims to enhance women’s access to agricultural markets, improve the competitiveness of women-led agribusinesses, and establish sustainable market linkages through innovation and technology and promote financial inclusion. The deadline for submission is August 31, 2025. Co-financing does not seem to be required, and applicants should be legally registered, rural women-led enterprises, as well as businesses with at least 30 per cent women in their leadership team..

ILO Eligibility.evidence: There are no applicant type restrictions.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: Selected entrepreneurs will receive tailored business coaching, seed funding for product development, support in market access, and networking opportunities.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: Seed funding for product development is offered, implying a grant size above USD 50,000.

Deadline is future.evidence: The deadline is August 31, 2025, which is in the future.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: Co-financing is not mentioned as a mandatory requirement.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: There is no mention of any specific accreditation requirement.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: There are no consortium leadership rules mentioned in the text.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: Eligibility is not restricted to applicants from the donor's domestic territory; it is focused on rural women-led enterprises in Somalia.

results.title: Somalia Women’s Market Access Innovation Challenge 2025
eligible_activities: Activities that enhance women’s access to agricultural markets
activities that improve the competitiveness of women-led agribusinesses
activities that establish sustainable market linkages through innovation and technology
results.proposal_summary: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD), together with the government of Somalia, is launching the Somalia Women’s Market Access Innovation Challenge 2025, seeking proposals from rural women entrepreneurs in Somalia. The challenge aims to enhance women’s access to agricultural markets, improve the competitiveness of women-led agribusinesses, and establish sustainable market linkages through innovation and technology and promote financial inclusion. Applicants should be legally registered, rural women-led enterprises, as well as businesses with at least 30 per cent women in their leadership team in Somalia. The deadline for submission is August 31, 2025. Seed funding, business coaching, and market access support will be provided. Co-financing is not explicitly required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on women's economic empowerment and market access in rural Somalia aligns with the ILO's goals of promoting decent work and reducing inequalities.

results.title: Call for External Chair of IFAD Replenishment Consultation
results.proposal_summary: IFAD seeks nominations for an External Chair to oversee the Consultation on the Fourteenth Replenishment of IFAD's Resources. The Chair will lead consultation sessions, facilitate discussions, and mobilize support for IFAD. Candidates should have knowledge of multilateral development banks, experience chairing international meetings, and demonstrated leadership in agricultural development and poverty reduction. The position is part-time from September/October 2025 to February 2027. The application deadline is June 22, 2025. This is not a grant opportunity.
results.ilo_match_evidence: IFAD's work in agricultural development and poverty reduction aligns with the ILO's goals of promoting decent work and sustainable livelihoods in rural areas, including social protection and employment.

results.title: Call for Proposals: Private Sector for Food Systems
eligible_activities: Strengthening partnerships between the private sector and smallholder producers
Stimulating investments in inclusive and climate-resilient agricultural value chains
results.proposal_summary: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is offering a grant to organizations in the private sector to strengthen partnerships with smallholder producers, stimulating investments in inclusive and climate-resilient agricultural value chains. The grant focuses on projects in Asia, specifically Cambodia, India, Laos, Philippines, Sri Lanka, and Vietnam. The application deadline was August 19, 2024, and required submission of a project proposal, budget, self-certification of eligibility, and a non-disclosure agreement. There's no mention of mandatory co-financing.

results.classification_summary: This is a call for proposals from IFAD for the Agri Connect project, aiming to enable private sector investment and access to services for smallholder farmers through a digital farmer registry. The total grant amount is up to US$1.5 million. Eligible applicants include consulting organizations, private sector entities, alliances of partner service providers, specialized NGOs, or inter-governmental organizations (including UN agencies). The deadline for submissions is August 16, 2024. Co-financing is required, with a minimum of 25% for private sector applicants and recommendations of at least 20% for other applicants.

results.title: IFAD Agri Connect Grant
results.proposal_summary: IFAD is offering a grant of up to US$1.5 million for the Agri Connect project, which aims to enable private sector investment and access to services for smallholder farmers through a digital farmer registry. Eligible applicants include consulting organizations, private sector entities, NGOs, and inter-governmental organizations. The deadline for submissions is August 16, 2024, and co-financing is required, with a minimum of 25% for private sector applicants and recommended 20% for others.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call focuses on enabling private sector investment and access to services for smallholder farmers, which aligns with the ILO's work on sustainable enterprises and employment creation in rural areas.
